title: capitanopsis angustifolia
intro:
Capitanopsis angustifolia, commonly known as Mexican caper, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is used in traditional medicine due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The therapeutic actions of Capitanopsis angustifolia include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
